mnemonics: SCARA - Self-Contained, Abstracted, Represents, Accessible, ACID
keyPoints: Self-Describing Nature: The database contains the data dictionary (metadata) that describes the data.
Insulation Between Programs and Data (Data Abstraction): Program-Data independence allows changes to data storage without affecting application programs.
Data Abstraction: Provides a conceptual representation of data, hiding details of how data is stored and maintained.
commonMistakes: Confusing logical data independence (change conceptual schema without affecting external views) with physical data independence (change internal schema without affecting conceptual view).
Assuming that just because data is in a DBMS, it is automatically secure and consistent without proper design and controls.
